old_claim.note: Vulcanized Rubber provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Durable rubber tire and belt materials supported practical road operation for Benz-style motor vehicles, but they were enabling components rather than the date-defining invention.

source_shape.source_locator: The development/specifications section describes steel-spoked wheels with solid rubber tires and a simple belt system serving as a single-speed transmission.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source identifies solid rubber tires and belt transmission as components of the Benz Patent-Motorwagen.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Those components support keeping vulcanized rubber as an enabling material edge while preventing it from being read as the core invention of the gas-engine vehicle.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: automobile_prototype remains enabled by vulcanized_rubber, now with component evidence.
would_reject_if: The automobile_prototype node were narrowed to a rubber-free internal-combustion demonstration rig.
A stronger source showed the early Benz road vehicle did not use any rubber tire or belt materials.
The vulcanized_rubber node were narrowed away from durable rubber components used in tires and belts.
short_reason: Benz-style prototypes used rubber tires and belt materials as enabling components.
